evidence.source_title: A prospective study of DWI, DCE-MRI and FDG PET imaging for target delineation in brachytherapy for cervical cancer.
evidence.raw_snippet:
PubMed ID: 27528120. 2016/09/01 00:00. PURPOSE: We examined the utility of dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI 
(DCE-MRI), diffusion-weighted MRI (DWI), and FDG-PET imaging for brachytherapy 
target delineation in patients with locally advanced cervical cancer.
MATERIALS AND METHODS: Twenty-two patients had DWI, DCE-MRI, and FDG-PET/CT 
scans after brachytherapy applicator insertion, in addition to standard 
T2-weighted (T2w) 3T MRI. Gross tumor volume (GTVB) and high-risk clinical 
target volume (HRC
